physicalDetails.detail: Human militia captain in grain-dusted leather, with a broken nose, tired eyes, and the look of a man worn thin by a problem nobody above him will name.
He belongs to the district physically: white powder on the gear, street habit in the stance, and the kind of local wear that comes from living above loading yards and counting houses.
Chapter 8 keeps Havel in district camouflage: shadow under an arch as a bored local officer, then all hard local habit inside alleys, service doors, and fallback shelters.

voiceMarkers.detail: Short, exact, and unsentimental; he speaks in district names, suspect habits, and what will or will not survive daylight as proof.
Dry enough to meet Sylvanas without flinching, but never wastes words on theater or rank polish.
His chapter 8 speech is the voice of a man translating city geometry into survival: target tells, route cuts, and the hard distinction between a search and a net.

beliefs.belief: The granary district is carrying disciplined men under civilian cover, even if the case is still pattern more than proof.
Local authority is not enough by itself; without better allies the truth will keep dying in daylight.
Andorhal has rehearsed this response for long enough that one fallback site is never enough.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed in chapter 8 when the first shelter is lost instantly and the second exists because Havel expected that possibility.

goalsHistory.active: Help the field party take a live operative tonight and turn months of suspicion into something that can finally hold.
goalsHistory.underlying: Protect Andorhal by breaking the hidden structure in his own district before it can shift or close around them.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Keep Andorhal usable after the breakthrough by splitting records, salting false trails, and holding the local end of the hunt with Corin.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Make the city costly to close cleanly while the core field team turns north and east toward the architect.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Tight-jawed, vindicated, and more useful now that somebody else can finally see what he has been seeing.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Vindicated, grim, and more alarmed than before; the chapter confirms he was right about the district and wrong about how deep the rehearsal went.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Chooses usefulness over ceremony when Corin brings the party in, giving them the district read instead of wasting time on rank games.
Turns over shift blocks, yard assignments, quiet hours, and suspect names so Sylvanas can map the cell at operational speed.
Commits himself to the same-night capture and starts placing the right men where their absence will not show.
Identifies the west-line target and the tell that makes him worth taking alive.
Opens the cooper's shed and then the escape chain of service spaces and fallback shelter without losing the team to obvious routes.
Commits to split caches and false trails instead of trying to accompany the main hunt beyond his local value.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Reads a working district by habit, identifying who drinks, who spends wages, who stands wrong in an apron, and which storehouses make no commercial sense.
Separates instinct from proof without losing either, giving Sylvanas a local map sharp enough to become a target package.
Operates as practical local command under pressure, translating district knowledge into doors, shifts, and capture windows.
Reads street behavior and worker routine well enough to mark the target in a living lane before Sylvanas ever closes on him.
Builds escape around city geometry rather than around hope, using service doors, storage runs, and fallback shelters like a second map of Andorhal.
Diagnoses district response at command level, recognizing that the city is running a net rather than an improvised search.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Corin
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses Havel as the street-side counterpart to the mage lane, bringing him in as the necessary second witness to the city.

howOthersAddressHim.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHim.pattern: Uses his surname directly once he proves he knows the district in operational detail.

howHeAddressesOthers.pattern: Begins bluntly with no false polish, then settles into direct cooperation once he recognizes competence.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Looks people over once, decides if they are useful, and moves on without ceremony.
Turns to the window too fast to hide how badly he needs the yard evidence to be real, then loses the last of his doubt when he sees it.
Uses the shape of the city with his whole body, never looking uncertain about which wall, yard, or hatch is still available.
Catches Corin by the arm without looking back, the motion showing how automatically he is already managing both route and weaker body in the formation.

knowledge.detail: Knows sickness has been clustering around the grain system for months.
Knows a new supplier pressure entered the city roughly three months ago.
Knows Teren and other yard workers are showing discipline that does not belong to ordinary labor.
Knows which quiet hours, lanes, and side doors make a live capture possible.
Learns the local cell has remote witness silencing and response teams ready before physical confirmation ever reaches them.
Sees Andorhal reclassified from hidden district sickness to the throat of a kingdom-scale rollout.
Adds the north-of-city fragments that help make Kel'Thuzad nameable.
Knows by chapter end that his job is local: keep the records alive, keep the city noisy with false leads, and let the four-woman unit travel on.

arcHistory.currentPosition: Chapter 7 introduces Havel as the ignored local militia witness who becomes Sylvanas’s human-structure guide inside Andorhal.

arc.lie: Pattern without proof is never going to be enough to move the city.
arc.truth: Once linked to the right allies and the residue proof, his district read becomes decisive.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter 8 moves Havel from ignored district witness to load-bearing local strategist. He is the man who knows which wall opens, which shelter matters, and which lies will keep the city busy after the hunters have already gone.
